Off Calendar Mar-09-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Arlo Uriarte, Esq. of LIBERATION LAW GROUP, PC, T:415.695.1000, for plaintiff/ moving party;
Lori V. Minassian, Esq. of DE CASTRO LAW GROUP, PC, T:310.270.9879, for defendant, on Court Call.

RULING: Hearing held. After hearing, for good cause and to the satisfaction of the Court, PLAINTIFFS CHRISTION GLOVER, MELINDA SORIANO, MARIA VICEDOR, TIFFANY GOMEZ's Motion For Approval Of Paga Settlement is GRANTED.

The Court further orders that the cover letter to the class members should explain the IRS position as discussed at the hearing.

A compliance hearing is set for July 7, 2020 at 9:30am in Department 302.

Order signed.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-30-2019 Continued To Nov-13-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Individual, Non-Class Arbitration And Stay
Off Calendar Mar-05-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jose Manuel DeCastro, Esq. 310-270-9877 of DeCastro Law Group for Defendant Aviation Services, L.P. through Court Call.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT HALLMARK AVIATION SERVICES, L.P.'S Motion To Compel Individual, Non-Class Arbitration And Stay Proceedings as to Plaintiffs' Private Attorney General Act Claim.

Hallmark Aviation Services, L.P.'s motion to compel arbitration of the individual, non-class claims asserted by plaintiffs Christine Glover, Melinda Soriano, Maria Vicedor, and Tiffany Gomez is granted, and the action is stayed pending the completion of arbitration. Hallmark Aviation has met its burden to show by a preponderance of the evidence that plaintiffs electronically signed the New Hire Policy Acknowledgement Form by completing an onboarding process which Plaintiffs accessed through a unique user name and password provided to them through their personal email accounts. (See Espejo v. Southern California Permanente Medical Group (2016) 246 Cal.App.4th 1047, 1062 [declaration authenticating electronic signature established existence of agreement to arbitrate]; Cal. Civ. Code 1633.9(a); Kim Decl. 4-6, 11, Exhs. E-J). By signing and agreeing to the New Hire Policy Acknowledgement Form, Plaintiffs acknowledged that they had reviewed and understood each separately referenced document, including Hallmark Aviation's Alternative Dispute Resolution Policy, a separate document accessible for review and download online. "The fact that [plaintiff] either chose not to read or take the time to understand these provisions is legally irrelevant." (Harris v. TAP Worldwide, LLC (2016) 248 Cal.App.4th 373, 383.) (see word minutes for full details)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
